_meta.title: Garo Language Master Dictionary
_meta.rakka_note: The rakka (·) is a phonetic marker belonging to the root word. Suffixes are always clean strings.
_meta.description: Trilingual dictionary: English, Garo, Hindi — with classifier engine, verb conjugations, grammar patterns, and conversational phrases

classifier_engine._description: Garo noun classifiers. Attach when counting nouns only.

word_order_sov.notes: · from root; suffixes are a and enga
· from root; suffixes are a, enga, aha
Both · marks are from root re·ang; suffix is just a or enga
· from root on·; suffixes are a, bo, enga

word_order_sov.garo: Rua (⚠ also means 'to pour' as verb)

word_order_sov.notes: Damo = common spoken wait; Sengbo also valid
Hai = expression particle for collective action
All three map to same Garo word
Don't see only with your eyes

word_order_sov.english: A good person is known by deeds
The forest gives, the forest takes
A little is better than nothing
In which room did you sleep?
He wrote well in the exam.
What is written in the letter?
Why do you drive the car?
Why did she pay the money?
Why did he bring the bag?
I saw the film last week.
word_order_sov.garo: Ang·a ia film ko nia mija antio.
word_order_sov.notes: Mija antio = last week; nia = saw

word_order_sov.english: What is written in the letter?
Why did you tell him to go?
Why did he bring the bag?
Why did she pay the money?
Why do you drive the car?
Why are they late for the meeting?
word_order_sov.garo: Bisong maina meeting ona ru·ute reba·a?

word_order_sov.english: How many apples are there in my hand?
How much did he pay you?
In which room did you sleep?
Which Indian state has the largest population?
word_order_sov.garo: India ni badiya adoko mande bangbata?

word_order_sov.english: Can you give me your pen?
word_order_sov.garo: Na·a nang pen ko on·a mangenma?

word_order_sov.notes: Hoe = yes; mangenva = of course / certainly
Distal demonstrative — differs from Ua (that, nearby)
Baitarong = how many; Baita = how much
Suffix: ang·ming = with me; nangming = with you
In the letter — chitthio = letter+location suffix
re·ang·a (went), cha·a (ate), tusi·a (slept) — · is part of each root, suffix is a
chaman·aha — the · is part of root chaman·, suffix is aha
chagen (will eat), reangen (will come), khuligen (will open)
Hai cha·ha (let's eat), Hai re·naha (let's go)
Angna (to me), Nangna (to you), Una (to him/her)
baitarong ra·a? (how many did you take?)
bagita reba·a? (which way did you come?)

word_order_sov.garo: Books, paper, leaves, flat / thin objects

word_order_sov.notes: fruits, vegetables, household items, clothing, kitchen, transport

giving_and_requesting.notes: Polite request with ka·sapae at end

commands_and_polite_requests.notes: Both valid; Damo is common spoken form

location_suffix_chi.english: At the market / To the market

rakka_note.note: The rakka (·) is part of the root word, NOT a suffix. Suffixes are clean strings — never prepend · to a suffix. e.g. root=re·ang + suffix=a → re·ang·a

king.description: books, paper, leaves, flat thin objects

verbs_present._description: Verbs in present/infinitive form. Root + suffix system applies.

verb_conjugations._description: Full conjugation tables for core Garo verbs

dak·na.english: to do / to work / to make

word_order.note: Never translate English SVO word order directly into Garo

recipient.rule: Add -na suffix to pronoun/noun for indirect object

ambiguous_words._description: Words with multiple meanings depending on context

household_items.axe: Rua (⚠ also means 'to pour' as verb)

wait a moment.notes: Damo = common spoken wait; Sengbo also valid

let's eat.notes: Hai = expression particle for collective action

look / see / watch.notes: All three map to same Garo word

don't judge by appearance.notes: Don't see only with your eyes

Ang·a ia film ko nia mija antio.

question_patterns.why are they late for the meeting?: Bisong maina meeting ona ru·ute reba·a?
question_patterns.which indian state has the largest popul: India ni badiya adoko mande bangbata?

can you give me your pen?.garo: Na·a nang pen ko on·a mangenma?

yes, of course..notes: Hoe = yes; mangenva = of course / certainly

that (far/there).notes: Distal demonstrative — differs from Ua (that, nearby)

how many / how much.notes: Baitarong = how many; Baita = how much

with (me / you).notes: Suffix: ang·ming = with me; nangming = with you

letter (written).notes: In the letter — chitthio = letter+location suffix
